title: Render Java Strategy Pattern (interface + N concretes + context)
description: Generates a Java Strategy pattern: an interface, one or more concrete strategy classes, and a context class that holds a swappable strategy. Uses the @@context_class@@ and @@package_name@@ placeholders.

parameters.name: context_class
parameters.description: PascalCase name of the context class (also used to derive the Strategy interface name).

parameters.name: package_name
parameters.description: Java package name, e.g. com.example.patterns.strategy.

title: Render TypeScript Strategy Pattern (interface + N concretes + context)
description: Generates a TypeScript Strategy pattern: an interface, one or more concrete strategy classes, and a context class. Includes a Vitest suite. Uses @@context_class@@ placeholder.

parameters.name: context_class
parameters.description: PascalCase name of the context class (also used to derive the Strategy interface name).

title: Render Python Strategy Pattern (Protocol/ABC + N concretes + context)
description: Generates a Python Strategy pattern using Protocol: a strategy protocol, one or more concrete strategy classes, a context class, and a pytest test module. Uses the @@context_class@@ placeholder.

parameters.name: context_class
parameters.description: PascalCase name of the context class (also used to derive the Strategy protocol name).

parameters.name: module_name
parameters.description: Python module base name (snake_case), e.g. order_strategy.

title: Render Python Function Stub from Signature
description: Renders a deterministic Python function stub (signature + docstring + NotImplementedError body) from a structured signature payload. Used by planning to seed executable code skeletons before the worker fills the body.

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: Snake_case function name to render.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: List of 'name:type' argument strings.

parameters.name: return_type
parameters.description: Return type annotation; default 'None'.

parameters.name: docstring
parameters.description: Docstring body (without triple quotes).

parameters.name: indent
parameters.description: Indentation width in spaces.

steps.name: validate_signature
steps.description: Ensure function_name matches python identifier rules and args list parses.

steps.name: render_signature
steps.description: Compose 'def <name>(<args>) -> <return_type>:' with the configured indent.

steps.name: render_body
steps.description: Emit indented docstring and 'raise NotImplementedError("...")' body.

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Rendered source must parse via ast.parse without errors.
No imports are emitted — caller decides imports.
acceptance_gates: ast.parse(rendered_source) succeeds
rendered_source contains 'def <function_name>' exactly once
rendered_source ends with 'raise NotImplementedError'

examples.name: simple_add
must_contain: def add(a: int, b: int) -> int:

title: Emit Sequential Workflow Definition
description: Emits a hub-recognizable workflow definition JSON for a sequence of N named steps with deterministic IDs. Language-agnostic: same shape works for Python/Java/TS tasks.

parameters.name: workflow_name
parameters.description: Workflow identifier (snake_case).

parameters.name: step_names
parameters.description: Ordered list of step display names.

parameters.name: depends_on_prev
parameters.description: If true, each step depends on the previous one (linear chain).

steps.name: build_steps
steps.description: For each name, mint a step_id 'step_<index>_<slugified_name>' and assign order index.

steps.name: wire_dependencies
steps.description: When depends_on_prev is true, each step's depends_on references the previous step_id.

steps.name: emit_workflow
steps.description: Serialize the workflow with name, version, and steps to a JSON object.

invariants: step_id is unique within the workflow.
No step depends on a forward step (no cycles).
Output JSON sorts keys lexicographically for stable diffs.
acceptance_gates: len(steps) == len(step_names)
All step_ids match pattern ^step_[0-9]+_[a-z0-9_]+$
depends_on_prev=True implies steps[i].depends_on == [steps[i-1].step_id] for i>0

title: Generate Vitest Scaffold from Contract
description: Generates a vitest spec file skeleton (describe + it blocks, AAA comments, placeholder assertions) for a TypeScript contract or component name.

parameters.name: subject_name
parameters.description: Name of the function/class/component under test.

parameters.name: behavior_count
parameters.description: Number of 'it' blocks to scaffold.

parameters.name: imports
parameters.description: List of import strings to emit at the top.

steps.name: emit_imports
steps.description: Emit the provided import lines plus default vitest imports (describe, it, expect).

steps.name: scaffold_describe
steps.description: Emit a describe(subject_name, () => { ... }) block with behavior_count it() entries.

steps.name: insert_placeholders
steps.description: Each it() gets AAA comments and a single expect(true).toBe(true) placeholder.

invariants: Output is valid TypeScript that tsc --noEmit accepts when placeholders are replaced.
behavior_count >= 1.
No it() block is empty.
acceptance_gates: Output contains exactly one describe() call
Output contains behavior_count it() calls
Output imports describe, it, expect from 'vitest'

title: Wrap CLI Invocation with Retry and Log
description: Wraps an arbitrary CLI command in a retry loop with exponential backoff and structured logging. Language-agnostic — produces a shell script or a callable wrapper contract.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The CLI command (single string) to wrap.

parameters.name: max_attempts
parameters.description: Number of retry attempts (>=1).

parameters.name: backoff_seconds
parameters.description: Initial backoff seconds; doubled per attempt.

parameters.name: log_file
parameters.description: Where to append the structured retry log lines.

steps.name: validate_command
steps.description: Reject empty command or command containing 'rm -rf /' (safety invariant).

steps.name: loop_with_backoff
steps.description: For attempt in 1..max_attempts: run command, on non-zero exit, sleep backoff * 2^(attempt-1), retry.

steps.name: log_outcome
steps.description: Append a single JSON line with timestamp, command, attempt, exit_code to log_file.

invariants: Refuses commands matching the deny-pattern list (rm -rf /, :(){:|:&};:, mkfs).
Logs are append-only — never truncates an existing log_file.
Total wall-clock time is bounded by max_attempts * sum(backoff_seconds * 2^i).
acceptance_gates: Empty command is rejected at validation step
Successful first attempt produces exactly one log line
Failed final attempt produces max_attempts log lines

title: Default-Deny Capability Gate
description: Java class that gates a capability behind an explicit allow-list. Default-deny: any capability not in the allow-list throws SecurityException. Used to enforce least-privilege in worker code.

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: Name of the gate class (PascalCase).

parameters.name: allowed_capabilities
parameters.description: List of capability strings that are explicitly allowed.

parameters.name: package_name
parameters.description: Java package for the generated class.

steps.name: emit_package_and_imports
steps.description: Emit 'package <name>;' and any required imports (java.util.Set, java.util.HashSet).

steps.name: emit_allow_set
steps.description: Generate a private static final Set<String> ALLOWED initialized from allowed_capabilities.

steps.name: emit_check_method
steps.description: Emit public static void require(String capability) that throws SecurityException when capability not in ALLOWED.

invariants: ALLOWED is populated at class-load time and never mutated.
require() throws SecurityException for any capability not in ALLOWED (default-deny).
Empty allowed_capabilities is permitted and means 'deny everything'.
acceptance_gates: Generated source compiles (javac) without warnings
require(unknownCapability) throws SecurityException at runtime
require(allowedCapability) returns normally

title: Render Mermaid UML Class Diagram (UML2-conformant)
description: Renders a Mermaid `classDiagram` block from a structured class/relationship payload. Uses UML2 notation: visibility (`+`/`-`/`#`/`~`), stereotypes (`<<interface>>`), abstract members (`{abstract}`), multiplicity on association ends, and the canonical UML relationship arrows (`<|--` inheritance, `*--` composition, `o--` aggregation, `-->` association, `..|>` realization, `..>` dependency, `--` plain link). Output is deterministic and round-trippable through any Mermaid 10.x renderer.

parameters.name: diagram_title
parameters.description: Optional diagram title shown above the diagram block.

parameters.name: classes
parameters.description: List of class definitions. Each entry is a JSON-encoded dict with `name`, optional `stereotype` (e.g. "interface", "abstract"), optional `fields` list of `name:type`, optional `methods` list of `name(args):type`. The renderer parses each entry.

parameters.name: relationships
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded relationship dicts with keys: `type` (inheritance|composition|aggregation|association|realization|dependency|link), `from`, `to`, optional `from_label`, `to_label` (for multiplicity), optional `label`.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Diagram direction.

steps.name: validate_classes
steps.description: Each class entry must parse as JSON with a non-empty `name`. Names must match `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$`. Duplicates are rejected.

steps.name: validate_relationships
steps.description: Each relationship must reference an existing class. Unknown arrow type is rejected.

steps.name: render_classes
steps.description: Emit `classDiagram` header, optional direction, optional title, then one `class <Name>{ <<stereotype>> field:type method(args):type }` block per class. Visibility markers (+/-/#/~) are prepended automatically for non-public members when the class carries the stereotype `interface` (methods are abstract by default).

steps.name: render_relationships
steps.description: Emit one relationship per line using the canonical arrow forms. Multiplicity labels are wrapped in quotes.

steps.name: emit_diagram
steps.description: Join blocks with newlines and return the final Mermaid source.

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs (class/relationship ordering is preserved as supplied).
Every relationship references a class declared in `classes`.
Output parses with Mermaid 10.x classDiagram grammar.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `classDiagram`
Output contains one `class <Name>` block per class
Output contains one canonical arrow per relationship
No relationship references an unknown class

title: Render Mermaid UML Sequence Diagram
description: Renders a Mermaid `sequenceDiagram` block from a structured participant/message/fragment payload. Supports typed lifelines (participant/actor), activation (`+`/`-`), sync/async/return messages, and combined fragments (`alt`, `par`, `loop`, `opt`, `critical`). Output is deterministic.

parameters.name: diagram_title
parameters.description: Optional diagram title.

parameters.name: participants
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded participant dicts with `id`, `label`, and optional `kind` (participant|actor|boundary|control|entity|database).

parameters.name: messages
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded message dicts with `from`, `to`, `text`, optional `type` (sync|async|return) and optional `activate` (bool).

parameters.name: fragments
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded fragment dicts with `type` (alt|par|loop|opt|critical), `label`, optional `condition`, and `messages` (nested message list, possibly with further nested fragments for branches).

parameters.name: autonumber
parameters.description: If true, prepend `autonumber` directive.

steps.name: validate_participants
steps.description: Each participant must have a unique non-empty id matching `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$`. Label is optional but must be a string if present.

steps.name: validate_messages
steps.description: Every message `from`/`to` must reference a known participant id.

steps.name: validate_fragments
steps.description: Fragments must use a supported type and have a label. Nested fragments are validated recursively.

steps.name: render_participants
steps.description: Emit `sequenceDiagram` header, optional autonumber, then one `participant <id> as <label>` or `actor <id> as <label>` per participant, in declared order.

steps.name: render_messages
steps.description: Emit message lines using the arrow form for the message type (`->>` sync, `--)` async, `-->>` return, `-->` reply). Activation is encoded by surrounding activate/deactivate pairs.

steps.name: render_fragments
steps.description: Emit combined-fragment blocks (alt/par/loop/opt/critical) wrapping their inner message lists and nested fragments. Branches of `alt` are emitted as `else <condition>` lines.

steps.name: emit_diagram
steps.description: Join sections with newlines and return the final source.
steps.input: participant lines, message lines, fragment lines

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Every message references a known participant.
Nested fragments are properly opened and closed (balanced `alt`/`end`, `par`/`end`, etc.).
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `sequenceDiagram`
Output contains one participant declaration per input participant
Output contains one message arrow per input message
Fragment open/close markers are balanced

title: Render Mermaid UML State Machine Diagram
description: Renders a Mermaid `stateDiagram-v2` block from a structured state/transition payload. Supports initial pseudostate (`[*] -->`), final pseudostate (`-->[*]`), simple states, composite states with nested substates, transitions with optional `event` and `guard` labels. Output is deterministic.

parameters.name: diagram_title
parameters.description: Optional diagram title.

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded state dicts with `id` and optional `label`. A state with `nested` (list of nested state ids) becomes a composite state.

parameters.name: transitions
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded transition dicts with `from`, `to`, optional `event`, optional `guard`. Use `from = "[*]"` for the initial pseudostate and `to = "[*]"` for the final pseudostate.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Diagram direction.

steps.name: validate_states
steps.description: Each state id must match `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$` and be unique. Composite states declare a non-empty `nested` list of known child state ids.

steps.name: validate_transitions
steps.description: Every transition `from` and `to` must reference a known state id or the initial/final pseudostate marker `[*]`. Duplicate transitions are rejected.

steps.name: render_states
steps.description: Emit `stateDiagram-v2` header, optional direction, then one `state "<label>" as <id>` per named state. Composite states emit a nested block with their substates.

steps.name: render_transitions
steps.description: Emit one `<from> --> <to> : <event> [<guard>]` line per transition. The pseudostate `[*]` is preserved verbatim.

steps.name: emit_diagram
steps.description: Join sections with newlines and return the final source.

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Every transition references a known state or the pseudostate marker.
Composite state nesting is properly closed.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `stateDiagram-v2`
Output contains at least one initial transition containing `[*] -->`
Output contains one transition line per input transition
Composite state blocks are balanced

title: Render Mermaid Use-Case Diagram
description: Renders a Mermaid `flowchart` block emulating the UML2 use-case view: actors as stadium-shaped nodes, the system as a subgraph boundary, use-cases as ellipses, with `<<include>>` and `<<extend>>` dependencies as dashed arrows. Output is deterministic.

parameters.name: diagram_title
parameters.description: Optional diagram title.

parameters.name: system_name
parameters.description: Name of the system boundary.

parameters.name: actors
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded actor dicts with `id`, `label`.

parameters.name: use_cases
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded use-case dicts with `id`, `label`.

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded association dicts: `{"actor": "...", "use_case": "..."}`.

parameters.name: includes
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded `<<include>>` dicts: `{"from": "...", "to": "..."}`.

parameters.name: extends
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded `<<extend>>` dicts: `{"from": "...", "to": "..."}`.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Diagram direction.

steps.name: validate_actors
steps.description: Each actor id must match `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$` and be unique.

steps.name: validate_use_cases
steps.description: Each use-case id must be a valid identifier and unique.

steps.name: validate_links
steps.description: Associations, includes and extends must reference known actors and use-cases.
steps.input: associations, includes, extends, validated actor list, validated use-case list

steps.name: render_actors
steps.description: Emit `flowchart <direction>` header, then each actor as a stadium node `id(["label"])`.

steps.name: render_system
steps.description: Emit a `subgraph <System>` boundary containing all use-case nodes shaped as ellipses `id([("label")])`.

steps.name: render_links
steps.description: Emit plain solid arrows for associations, dashed arrows with `<<include>>` and `<<extend>>` labels.

steps.name: emit_diagram
steps.description: Join sections with newlines and return the final source.
steps.input: actor lines, system subgraph, link lines

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Every association/includes/extends references a known actor or use-case.
System subgraph is properly opened and closed.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `flowchart`
Output contains a `subgraph` block named after `system_name`
Output contains one stadium node per actor and one ellipse per use-case
Includes/extends carry the corresponding stereotype label

title: Render Mermaid Activity Diagram
description: Renders a Mermaid `flowchart` block emulating UML2 activity notation: initial node, action nodes, decision diamonds, fork/join bars, and final node. Output is deterministic.

parameters.name: diagram_title
parameters.description: Optional diagram title.

parameters.name: nodes
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded node dicts with `id`, `label`, `shape` (action|decision|fork|join|initial|final|merge).

parameters.name: edges
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded edge dicts with `from`, `to`, optional `label`. Edges from decision nodes use `label` as the branch condition (e.g. `[yes]`, `[no]`).

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Diagram direction.

steps.name: validate_nodes
steps.description: Each node id must match `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$` and be unique. Shape must be one of action|decision|fork|join|initial|final|merge.

steps.name: validate_edges
steps.description: Every edge `from`/`to` must reference a known node id.

steps.name: render_nodes
steps.description: Emit `flowchart <direction>` header, then render each node using the appropriate Mermaid shape: `id(["label"])` for fork/join bars, `id{{"label"}}` for decision diamonds, `id(("label"))` for initial/final circles, `id["label"]` for actions, `id>"label"]` for merge.

steps.name: render_edges
steps.description: Emit one `<from> -->|<label>| <to>` line per edge. Edge labels from decision nodes use `|condition|` syntax.

steps.name: emit_diagram
steps.description: Join sections with newlines and return the final source.

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Every edge references a known node.
Exactly one initial node and at least one final node exist.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `flowchart`
Output contains at least one initial node circle
Output contains at least one final node circle
Output contains one edge line per input edge

title: Render BPMN 2.0 Process Diagram (single process)
description: Renders a deterministic BPMN 2.0 XML document for a single process: start/end events, tasks (user/service/script/manual task variants), exclusive/inclusive/parallel gateways, and sequence flows. Output conforms to the OMG BPMN 2.0 `bpmn:definitions` namespace `http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL` and parses with any compliant BPMN engine (Camunda, bpmn.io, etc.).

parameters.name: definitions_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the bpmn:definitions root (e.g. `Definitions_1`).

parameters.name: process_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the bpmn:process (e.g. `Process_OrderFulfillment`).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: Human-readable process name.

parameters.name: elements
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded flow element dicts. Supported types: `startEvent`, `endEvent`, `userTask`, `serviceTask`, `scriptTask`, `manualTask`, `exclusiveGateway`, `inclusiveGateway`, `parallelGateway`. Each element carries `id`, optional `name`, plus type-specific fields.

parameters.name: flows
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded sequence flow dicts with `id`, `sourceRef`, `targetRef`, optional `name`, optional `conditionExpression` (for exclusive gateway outgoing flows).

steps.name: validate_elements
steps.description: Every element id must be unique and match `^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$`. Exactly one `startEvent` is required. Each element must declare its required type-specific fields.

steps.name: validate_flows
steps.description: Every flow's `sourceRef` and `targetRef` must reference a known element id. Duplicate flow ids are rejected.

steps.name: render_definitions
steps.description: Emit `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`, then `<bpmn:definitions id="..." xmlns:bpmn="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/MODEL" xmlns:bpmndi="http://www.omg.org/spec/BPMN/20100524/DI" targetNamespace="http://bpmn.io/schema/bpmn">`.

steps.name: render_process
steps.description: Emit `<bpmn:process id="..." name="..." isExecutable="true">` followed by element blocks and sequence flows, then `</bpmn:process>`.
steps.input: process_id, process_name, validated element list, validated flow list

steps.name: render_diagram
steps.description: Emit a minimal `<bpmndi:BPMNDiagram>` with one `<bpmndi:BPMNPlane>` and one `<bpmndi:BPMNShape>`/`<bpmndi:BPMNEdge>` per element and flow, all positioned on a deterministic 100x100 grid in declaration order. This keeps the file openable in any BPMN modelling tool.
steps.input: validated element list, validated flow list

steps.name: finalize
steps.description: Close the definitions and return the full XML string.
steps.input: definitions header, process block, diagram block

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Output parses as well-formed XML.
Every flow `sourceRef`/`targetRef` references an element declared in `elements`.
Exactly one `startEvent` exists.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `<?xml`
Output contains a single `bpmn:process` element
Output contains one `bpmn:startEvent`, one `bpmn:endEvent` minimum
Output contains one `bpmn:sequenceFlow` per input flow
Output validates against the BPMN 2.0 XSD when parsed

title: Render BPMN 2.0 Process with Pool and Lanes
description: Renders a BPMN 2.0 XML document for a single process wrapped in a `bpmn:laneSet` containing one or more `bpmn:lane`s. Each lane assigns flow elements via `flowNodeRef`. Output conforms to the OMG BPMN 2.0 namespace and parses with compliant engines.

parameters.name: definitions_id
parameters.description: Definitions root id.

parameters.name: process_id
parameters.description: Process id.

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: Process name.

parameters.name: elements
parameters.description: List of flow elements (same shape as `bpmn.process`).

parameters.name: flows
parameters.description: List of sequence flows (same shape as `bpmn.process`).

parameters.name: lanes
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded lane dicts with `id`, `name`, and `flow_node_refs` (list of element ids assigned to this lane). Lanes are emitted in declared order; each flow element must be assigned to exactly one lane.

steps.name: validate_elements
steps.description: Validate elements (same rules as `bpmn.process`).

steps.name: validate_lanes
steps.description: Each lane id must be unique. The union of all `flow_node_refs` across lanes must cover exactly the set of element ids (no orphans, no duplicates).

steps.name: validate_flows
steps.description: Validate flows against the validated elements.

steps.name: render_process
steps.description: Emit definitions, then process, then a `bpmn:laneSet` with the lanes and their `flowNodeRef`s.
steps.input: validated element list, validated flow list, validated lane list

steps.name: render_diagram
steps.description: Emit a minimal BPMN-DI block laying out elements horizontally lane-by-lane.
steps.input: validated element list, validated flow list, validated lane list

steps.name: finalize
steps.description: Close definitions and return the XML string.

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Output parses as well-formed XML.
The set of element ids equals the set of `flow_node_refs` across all lanes.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `<?xml`
Output contains exactly one `bpmn:laneSet`
Output contains one `bpmn:lane` per input lane
Every element id appears in exactly one lane's `flowNodeRef`

title: Render BPMN 2.0 Collaboration (multi-pool with message flows)
description: Renders a BPMN 2.0 `bpmn:collaboration` containing two or more `bpmn:participant` (pool) processes and one or more `bpmn:messageFlow`s between them. Each participant embeds its own process with the same element/flow shapes as `bpmn.process`. Output conforms to the OMG BPMN 2.0 namespace.

parameters.name: definitions_id
parameters.description: Definitions root id.

parameters.name: participants
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded participant dicts with `id`, `name`, `process_id`, `process_name`, `elements` (list), `flows` (list), optional `lanes` (list, same shape as `bpmn.pool_lane`). Each participant must reference a unique process id.

parameters.name: message_flows
parameters.description: List of JSON-encoded message flow dicts with `id`, `name`, `source_ref` (participant id), `target_ref` (participant id). Source/target must reference declared participants.

steps.name: validate_participants
steps.description: Each participant id and process_id must be unique. Each participant must carry at least one startEvent and one endEvent across its elements.

steps.name: validate_message_flows
steps.description: Every message flow references declared participants; duplicate message flow ids are rejected.

steps.name: render_processes
steps.description: Emit one `bpmn:process` per participant (with optional laneSet), in declared order.

steps.name: render_collaboration
steps.description: Emit `<bpmn:collaboration id="Collaboration_1">` containing one `bpmn:participant` per participant process and the `bpmn:messageFlow`s.
steps.input: validated participant list, validated message-flow list

steps.name: render_diagram
steps.description: Emit a minimal BPMN-DI block laying out each participant as a horizontal pool with its internal elements and message flows as edges.
steps.input: validated participant list, validated message-flow list

steps.name: finalize
steps.description: Wrap everything in `bpmn:definitions` and return the XML string.
steps.input: process blocks, collaboration block, diagram block

invariants: Output is byte-deterministic for identical inputs.
Output parses as well-formed XML.
Every participant id is referenced by its process and at most one message flow endpoint per side.
acceptance_gates: Output starts with `<?xml`
Output contains exactly one `bpmn:collaboration`
Output contains one `bpmn:participant` per input participant
Output contains one `bpmn:messageFlow` per input message flow
